questions.question: A shopkeeper buys an article for Rs. 400 and sells it for Rs. 500. Find the profit percentage.
A can do a work in 10 days and B in 15 days. Working together, in how many days will they finish it?
questions.explanation: Combined one-day work = 1/10 + 1/15 = 3/30 + 2/30 = 5/30 = 1/6. So they finish in 6 days.

questions.question: Statements: All cats are dogs. All dogs are animals. Which conclusion follows? 'All cats are animals.'
questions.explanation: Since all cats are dogs and all dogs are animals, all cats must be animals. The conclusion follows.

questions.question: If A > B and B > C, which of the following is definitely true?
questions.explanation: If A > B and B > C, then by transitivity A > C.

questions.question: Spot the error: 'He (A)/ don't (B)/ like coffee (C)/ No error (D)'
questions.explanation: With the singular subject 'He', the correct form is 'doesn't', not 'don't'.

questions.question: The repo rate in India is decided by which institution?
questions.explanation: The repo rate is decided by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) through its Monetary Policy Committee.

questions.question: Which of the following is the minimum paid-up equity capital required for setting up a Small Finance Bank (SFB) in India, as per the revised RBI guidelines?
questions.explanation: As per the Reserve Bank of India guidelines, the minimum paid-up equity capital for Small Finance Banks (SFBs) has been increased from Rs. 100 crore to Rs. 200 crore to ensure their financial robustness.

questions.question: What percentage of Adjusted Net Bank Credit (ANBC) or Credit Equivalent Amount of Off-Balance Sheet Exposure, whichever is higher, is the Priority Sector Lending (PSL) target for Domestic Scheduled Commercial Banks in India?
questions.explanation: Domestic Scheduled Commercial Banks (excluding RRBs and SFBs) are mandated by the RBI to allocate 40% of their Adjusted Net Bank Credit (ANBC) or Credit Equivalent Amount of Off-Balance Sheet Exposure, whichever is higher, to the priority sector.

questions.question: What is the revised annual premium payable by the subscriber under the Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY), effective from June 1, 2022?
questions.explanation: The annual premium for the Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY), an accident insurance scheme, was revised from Rs. 12 to Rs. 20 per annum with effect from June 1, 2022.

questions.question: Which Article of the Constitution of India mandates the presentation of the 'Annual Financial Statement' (commonly known as the Union Budget) in Parliament?
questions.explanation: According to Article 112 of the Indian Constitution, the President shall in respect of every financial year cause to be laid before both the Houses of Parliament a statement of the estimated receipts and expenditure of the Government of India, referred to as the 'Annual Financial Statement'.

questions.question: In which year did the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) commence its operations, and under which legislative Act was it established?
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, under the provisions of the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, following the recommendations of the Hilton Young Commission.

questions.question: Which of the following Indian states shares its geographical border with the maximum number of other Indian states?
questions.explanation: Uttar Pradesh shares its borders with the maximum number of states. It is bordered by 8 states (Uttarakhand, Himachal Pradesh, Haryana, Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, Bihar) and 1 Union Territory (Delhi).

questions.question: Which of the following vitamins is water-soluble and cannot be stored in the body for long periods?
questions.explanation: Vitamins are classified as either fat-soluble (A, D, E, K) or water-soluble (B-complex vitamins and Vitamin C). Water-soluble vitamins dissolve in water and are readily excreted in urine, meaning they must be consumed regularly.

questions.question: The headquarters of the Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank (AIIB) is located in which of the following cities?
questions.explanation: The Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank (AIIB) is a multilateral development bank headquartered in Beijing, China, which initiated its operations in January 2016.

questions.question: Under the Reserve Bank - Integrated Ombudsman Scheme, 2021, what is the maximum limit of compensation the Ombudsman can award for any loss directly suffered by the complainant?
questions.explanation: Under the Integrated Ombudsman Scheme, 2021, the Ombudsman has the power to award compensation up to Rs. 20 lakhs for any consequential financial loss directly suffered by the complainant, and up to Rs. 1 lakh for mental agony and harassment.

questions.question: Which of the following committees recommended the establishment of Regional Rural Banks (RRBs) in India to cater to rural credit needs?
questions.explanation: The Narasimham Working Group (1975) recommended the establishment of Regional Rural Banks (RRBs) to bridge the credit gap in rural areas. Consequently, the first five RRBs were set up on October 2, 1975.

questions.question: Which of the following is the minimum paid-up equity capital required for setting up a Small Finance Bank (SFB) in India, as per the latest RBI guidelines?
questions.explanation: As per the RBI's guidelines, the minimum paid-up voting equity capital for setting up a Small Finance Bank (SFB) has been increased from Rs. 100 crore to Rs. 200 crore.

questions.question: Which committee recommended the introduction of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) framework in India to decide policy rates?
questions.explanation: The Urjit Patel Committee (Committee to Revise and Strengthen the Monetary Policy Framework), which submitted its report in 2014, recommended the establishment of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India to bring transparency and accountability.

questions.question: Under the Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY), what is the annual premium payable by the subscriber for an accidental death and full disability cover of Rs. 2 Lakh?
questions.explanation: The annual premium for the Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY) was revised from Rs. 12 to Rs. 20 with effect from June 1, 2022. It offers an accidental death and full disability cover of Rs. 2 Lakh.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Constitution of India is the Annual Financial Statement (Union Budget) presented to the Parliament?
questions.explanation: Article 112 of the Indian Constitution deals with the Annual Financial Statement, which is popularly referred to as the Union Budget.

questions.question: Through which of the following Indian states does the Tropic of Cancer NOT pass?
questions.explanation: The Tropic of Cancer passes through 8 Indian states: Gujarat, Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, West Bengal, Tripura, and Mizoram. It does not pass through Odisha.

questions.question: During the Viceroyalty of whom was the Ancient Monuments Preservation Act passed in 1904?
questions.explanation: The Ancient Monuments Preservation Act was passed in 1904 during the tenure of Lord Curzon, who served as the Viceroy of India from 1899 to 1905.

questions.question: Which of the following diseases is caused by the deficiency of Vitamin D in children?
questions.explanation: Rickets is a skeletal disorder caused by a lack of vitamin D, calcium, or phosphate, primarily affecting growing children and leading to soft and weak bones.

questions.question: The head office of the International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD) is located in which city?
questions.explanation: The International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD) is a specialized agency of the United Nations dedicated to eradicating poverty and hunger in rural areas, headquartered in Rome, Italy.

questions.question: In the context of banking regulation in India, what does the acronym 'LEI' stand for, which is a 20-digit unique code used to identify parties to financial transactions globally?
questions.explanation: The Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) is a 20-character, alpha-numeric code based on the ISO 17442 standard. RBI has mandated LEI for non-individual entities undertaking high-value transactions to improve financial data systems.

questions.question: Which of the following indexes is published by the NITI Aayog to measure the performance of states and union territories in terms of water management?
questions.explanation: The Composite Water Management Index (CWMI) is launched by NITI Aayog to provide an annual snapshot of water management performance across Indian states and Union Territories.

questions.question: Under whose reign in the Delhi Sultanate was the dedicated department for agricultural development, 'Diwan-i-Amir-Kohi', established?
questions.explanation: Muhammad bin Tughlaq established the department of 'Diwan-i-Amir-Kohi' to bring uncultivated land under cultivation through direct state support and loans to farmers.

questions.question: Who among the following was the President of the Indian National Congress during the historic 1916 Lucknow Session, where the Lucknow Pact was signed?
questions.explanation: Ambica Charan Mazumdar presided over the 1916 Lucknow Session of the Indian National Congress, which marked the reunion of the moderate and extremist factions as well as the alliance with the Muslim League.

questions.question: The Barren Island, which contains the only active volcano in the Indian subcontinent, is located in which of the following water bodies?
questions.explanation: Barren Island is located in the Andaman Sea, which is a marginal sea of the northeastern Indian Ocean and part of the Bay of Bengal.

questions.question: Which of the following landlocked countries in South America is bordered exclusively by Brazil, Argentina, and Bolivia?
questions.explanation: Paraguay is one of the two landlocked nations in South America and shares its borders strictly with Bolivia to the northwest, Brazil to the northeast, and Argentina to the south and west.

questions.question: What is the scientific term used to describe the boundary zone between the Earth's crust and the upper mantle where seismic wave velocities change abruptly?
questions.explanation: The Mohorovicic Discontinuity, commonly called the Moho, is the boundary layer separating the Earth's crust from the underlying mantle.

questions.question: Which of the following laws of physics states that the total current entering a junction or node in an electrical circuit is equal to the total current leaving that node?
questions.explanation: Kirchhoff's Current Law (KCL) is based on the law of conservation of electric charge, stating that the algebraic sum of currents meeting at any junction is zero.

questions.question: What is the primary mineral constituent of 'Lapis Lazuli', a deep-blue metamorphic rock used extensively as a semi-precious stone?
questions.explanation: Lazurite is a blue feldspathoid silicate mineral and is the main component of the gemstone lapis lazuli, which gives it its signature rich blue color.

questions.question: Which of the following endocrine glands in the human body is referred to as the 'Master Gland' because it regulates the functions of other endocrine glands?
questions.explanation: The pituitary gland is called the 'master gland' because it secretes hormones that trigger other endocrine glands (like the thyroid, adrenal glands, and gonads) to produce their own hormones.

questions.question: The famous Battle of Talikota (1565 AD), which led to the collapse of the Vijayanagara Empire, was fought during the reign of which nominal Emperor of Vijayanagara?
questions.explanation: While the de facto control of the empire was in the hands of the prime minister Aliya Rama Raya (who was killed in the battle), the nominal Emperor of Vijayanagara at the time was Sadasiva Raya.

questions.question: In which year was the controversial 'Vernacular Press Act' enacted by Viceroy Lord Lytton to curtail the freedom of Indian-language newspapers?
questions.explanation: The Vernacular Press Act was passed in 1878 to suppress local-language newspapers that were critical of British policies, particularly the Second Anglo-Afghan War.

questions.question: A, B, and C enter into a partnership. A invests Rs. 24,000 for 8 months, B invests Rs. 30,000 for some months, and C invests Rs. 20,000 for 12 months. Out of a total annual profit of Rs. 56,000, B receives Rs. 20,000 as his share. For how many months did B invest his money?
questions.explanation:
Let B invest for x months.
Ratio of profit sharing of A, B, and C is based on the product of investment and time:
Ratio = (24000 * 8) : (30000 * x) : (20000 * 12)
= 192,000 : 30,000x : 240,000
Dividing by 6000:
Ratio = 32 : 5x : 40

Total profit share units = 32 + 5x + 40 = 72 + 5x
B's share of profit is Rs. 20,000 out of Rs. 56,000.
So, 5x / (72 + 5x) = 20000 / 56000
5x / (72 + 5x) = 5 / 14
x / (72 + 5x) = 1 / 14
14x = 72 + 5x
9x = 72
x = 8
Therefore, B invested for 8 months.

questions.question: A bag contains 5 red, 4 green, and 3 blue balls. If three balls are drawn at random from the bag, what is the probability that at least two of the drawn balls are red?
questions.explanation:
Total number of balls = 5 + 4 + 3 = 12.
Total number of ways to draw 3 balls from 12 = 12C3 = (12 * 11 * 10) / (3 * 2 * 1) = 220.

We need the probability of drawing at least 2 red balls. This comprises two cases:
Case 1: Exactly 2 red balls and 1 other ball (green or blue).
Ways = 5C2 * 7C1 = 10 * 7 = 70.

Case 2: Exactly 3 red balls.
Ways = 5C3 = 10.

Total favorable ways = 70 + 10 = 80.
Probability = 80 / 220 = 4/11.

questions.question: A train crosses a vertical pole in 12 seconds and a platform of length 240 meters in 28 seconds. What is the speed of the train in km/h?
questions.explanation: The train takes 12 seconds to cross its own length (L) and 28 seconds to cross L + 240 meters. This means it takes 16 seconds to cover 240 meters. The speed of the train is 240/16 = 15 m/s. Converting to km/h, we get 15 * (18/5) = 54 km/h.

questions.question: A rectangular plot has a length of 20 meters and a breadth of 15 meters. A path of uniform width 2.5 meters is built all around the outside of the plot. Find the cost of graveling this path at the rate of Rs. 12 per square meter.
questions.explanation: The inner area is 20 * 15 = 300 sq m. The outer dimensions including the 2.5 m path on all sides are 25 m by 20 m, giving an outer area of 500 sq m. The area of the path is 500 - 300 = 200 sq m. At Rs. 12 per sq m, the total cost is 200 * 12 = Rs. 2,400.

questions.question: A vessel contains a mixture of milk and water in the ratio 5 : 3. If 16 liters of this mixture is taken out and replaced with 16 liters of water, the ratio of milk to water becomes 3 : 5. Find the initial quantity of milk in the vessel.
questions.explanation: Let the initial quantity of milk and water be 5x and 3x respectively (Total = 8x). When 16 liters of mixture is removed: Milk removed = 16 * (5/8) = 10 liters; Water removed = 16 * (3/8) = 6 liters. Remaining milk = 5x - 10, remaining water = 3x - 6. After adding 16 liters of water, the new water quantity becomes 3x - 6 + 16 = 3x + 10. The new ratio is 3:5, so (5x - 10) / (3x + 10) = 3/5. Solving this: 5(5x - 10) = 3(3x + 10) => 25x - 50 = 9x + 30 => 16x = 80 => x = 5. The initial quantity of milk = 5x = 5 * 5 = 25 liters.

questions.question: Three pipes A, B, and C can fill a cistern in 12 hours, 15 hours, and 20 hours respectively. Pipe A is opened at 7:00 AM, Pipe B at 8:00 AM, and Pipe C at 10:00 AM. At what time will the cistern be completely full?
questions.explanation: Let the total capacity of the cistern be LCM(12, 15, 20) = 60 units. Efficiency of A = 5 units/hr, B = 4 units/hr, and C = 3 units/hr. From 7:00 AM to 8:00 AM (1 hour), only A is open: work done = 5 * 1 = 5 units. From 8:00 AM to 10:00 AM (2 hours), A and B are open: work done = (5 + 4) * 2 = 18 units. Total work done by 10:00 AM = 5 + 18 = 23 units. Remaining work = 60 - 23 = 37 units. From 10:00 AM onwards, all three pipes are open: combined efficiency = 5 + 4 + 3 = 12 units/hr. Time taken to fill the remaining part = 37 / 12 hours = 3 hours and 5 minutes. Therefore, the cistern will be full at 10:00 AM + 3 hours 5 minutes = 1:05 PM.

questions.question:
In the following question, some symbols are used with the following meanings:
'A @ B' means 'A is not smaller than B'
'A $ B' means 'A is not greater than B'
'A # B' means 'A is neither greater than nor equal to B'
'A % B' means 'A is neither smaller than nor equal to B'
'A & B' means 'A is neither greater than nor smaller than B'

Given Statements: P # Q, Q & R, R @ S
Which of the following conclusions is/are definitely true?
I. P # S
II. S $ Q
options: Both conclusions I and II are true
Neither conclusion I nor II is true
questions.explanation:
Decoding the symbols, we get: '@' means '>=', '$' means '<=', '#' means '<', '%' means '>', and '&' means '='. The given statements translate to: P < Q, Q = R, R >= S. Combining these, we get the relationship: P < Q = R >= S. 
For conclusion I (P # S -> P < S): Since the symbols between P and S change direction (< and >=), no definite relationship can be established. Thus, I is not definitely true.
For conclusion II (S $ Q -> S <= Q): Since Q = R and R >= S, we have Q >= S, which means S <= Q. Thus, II is definitely true.

questions.question:
Read the statements carefully and decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements, disregarding commonly known facts.

Statements:
1. Only a few Gliders are Parachutes.
2. No Parachute is a Balloon.
3. All Balloons are Airships.

Conclusions:
I. Some Gliders are definitely not Balloons.
II. Some Airships being Parachutes is a possibility.
options: Neither conclusion I nor II follows
Both conclusions I and II follow
questions.explanation:
Statement 1 ('Only a few Gliders are Parachutes') implies that 'Some Gliders are Parachutes' and 'Some Gliders are not Parachutes'. 
For Conclusion I: Since some Gliders are Parachutes, and no Parachute is a Balloon, those Gliders that are Parachutes can never be Balloons. Thus, 'Some Gliders are definitely not Balloons' is true.
For Conclusion II: While no Balloon can be a Parachute, Airships are a larger set containing Balloons. The part of Airships that does not overlap with Balloons can safely overlap with Parachutes without violating any statement. Hence, 'Some Airships being Parachutes is a possibility' is also true. Both conclusions follow.

questions.question:
Read the sentence to find out whether there is any grammatical error in it. The error, if any, will be in one part of the sentence. Mark that part as your answer.

'Hardly had the regulatory body (A) / initiated the forensic audit than (B) / several severe discrepancies in (C) / the ledger books came to light. (D)'
questions.explanation: The correlative conjunction 'Hardly' is paired with 'when' or 'before', whereas 'No sooner' is paired with 'than'. Therefore, 'than' in Part B must be replaced with 'when' to make the sentence grammatically correct.

questions.question:
Each sentence below contains two blanks. Choose the set of words for each blank that best fits the meaning of the sentence as a whole.

'The board of directors decided to _______ the proposed merger, fearing that it would _______ the company's core brand identity.'
questions.explanation: The word 'fearing' indicates that the consequence of the merger on the brand identity was negative. Hence, the second blank needs a negative word like 'compromise' or 'weaken'. If they feared this negative outcome, they would not approve ('endorse') it; instead, they would reject or postpone ('shelve') it. Thus, 'shelve, compromise' fits perfectly.

questions.question: What is the total Priority Sector Lending (PSL) target for Small Finance Banks (SFBs) in India, expressed as a percentage of their Adjusted Net Bank Credit (ANBC) or Credit Equivalent Amount of Off-Balance Sheet Exposure, whichever is higher?
questions.explanation: Small Finance Banks (SFBs) are required to allocate 75% of their Adjusted Net Bank Credit (ANBC) or Credit Equivalent Amount of Off-Balance Sheet Exposure, whichever is higher, to the priority sector, whereas for domestic commercial banks (excluding RRBs and SFBs), the target is 40%.

questions.question: The Imperial Bank of India, which was later transformed into the State Bank of India in 1955, was established in 1921 by amalgamating three Presidency Banks under which of the following Acts?
questions.explanation: The Imperial Bank of India was constituted under the Imperial Bank of India Act, 1920, and began operations on January 27, 1921, by amalgamating the three Presidency Banks: the Bank of Bengal, the Bank of Bombay, and the Bank of Madras.

questions.question: A man borrows Rs. 33,100 at 10% per annum compound interest, compounded annually. He agrees to pay back the loan in three equal annual installments. Find the value of each installment.
questions.explanation: Let the annual installment be x. The present value of all three installments must equal the borrowed amount. Therefore, P = x/(1 + r/100) + x/(1 + r/100)^2 + x/(1 + r/100)^3. Given P = 33100 and r = 10%, we get: 33100 = x/1.1 + x/1.21 + x/1.331 => 33100 = x * (1.21 + 1.1 + 1)/1.331 => 33100 = x * (3.31)/1.331 => 33100 = x * 3310/1331. Solving for x, we get x = (33100 * 1331) / 3310 = Rs. 13,310.

questions.question: A and B started a business by investing Rs. 40,000 and Rs. 50,000 respectively. After 4 months, C joined them with an investment of Rs. 60,000. Since A was the managing partner, he was paid a salary of Rs. 1,000 per month from the profit. If the total profit at the end of the year was Rs. 38,000, find B's share in the profit.
questions.explanation: First, calculate the ratio of profit-sharing based on investment and time: A's share = 40,000 * 12 = 4,80,000; B's share = 50,000 * 12 = 6,00,000; C's share = 60,000 * 8 = 4,80,000. The ratio of their shares is 4,80,000 : 6,00,000 : 4,80,000 = 4 : 5 : 4. A's annual salary is Rs. 1,000 * 12 = Rs. 12,000. The remaining profit to be distributed in the ratio is 38,000 - 12,000 = Rs. 26,000. B's share of this remaining profit is (5 / 13) * 26,000 = Rs. 10,000.

questions.question: Eight persons - P, Q, R, S, T, U, V, and W - are sitting around a circular table facing the center. P sits third to the left of Q. Two people sit between P and R. S sits second to the right of R. T is an immediate neighbor of S. U sits third to the right of V. How many people sit between U and P when counted from the right of U?
questions.explanation:
Let's map the positions of the 8 persons facing the center (positions 1 to 8 in counter-clockwise order):
1. Let Q be at position 1.
2. P sits third to the left of Q (clockwise), so P is at position 6.
3. Two people sit between P and R. Counting clockwise or counter-clockwise, R must be at position 3 (since position 1 is occupied by Q).
4. S sits second to the right of R (counter-clockwise), so S is at position 5.
5. T is an immediate neighbor of S. Since position 6 is occupied by P, T must be at position 4.
6. U sits third to the right of V. The remaining vacant positions are 2, 7, and 8. If V is at 7, then U (third to the right of V) is at position 2. This is valid. The remaining position 8 is occupied by W.

Now, we need to find the number of people between U (position 2) and P (position 6) when counting from the right of U (counter-clockwise): positions 3 (R), 4 (T), and 5 (S) lie between them. Thus, there are exactly 3 people.

questions.question: Point A is 6m West of Point B. Point C is 5m North of Point B. Point D is 11m East of Point C. Point E is 10m South of Point D. Point F is 5m West of Point E. What is the shortest distance between Point A and Point F, and in which direction is Point F with respect to Point A?
questions.explanation:
Let's resolve the coordinates of each point starting with A at the origin (0, 0):
- B is 6m East of A: B = (6, 0)
- C is 5m North of B: C = (6, 5)
- D is 11m East of C: D = (17, 5)
- E is 10m South of D: E = (17, -5)
- F is 5m West of E: F = (12, -5)

To find the shortest distance between A (0,0) and F (12, -5):
Distance = \sqrt{(12 - 0)^2 + (-5 - 0)^2} = \sqrt{144 + 25} = \sqrt{169} = 13m.
Since F is at (12, -5), it is to the East and South of A, which means F is in the South-East direction with respect to A.

questions.question:
In the following sentence, a part of the sentence is underlined. Choose the correct alternative that can replace the underlined part to make the sentence grammatically correct.

Sentence: No sooner had the delegation arrived at the capital *when the protests erupted* across the central square.
questions.explanation: The correlative conjunction 'No sooner' is always paired with 'than' (not 'when' or 'then'). The clause introduced by 'than' should be in the simple past tense ('the protests erupted') to indicate a sequence of events following the past perfect action ('had the delegation arrived').

questions.question:
In the following sentence, one of the parts contains a grammatical error. Identify the erroneous segment.

(A) The monetary policy committee's decision / (B) to keep repo rates unchanged / (C) was prompted by the need to support growth / (D) despite of persistent inflationary pressures.
questions.explanation: The preposition 'despite' must not be followed by 'of'. It is either 'despite persistent inflationary pressures' or 'in spite of persistent inflationary pressures'. Therefore, the error lies in part (D).

questions.question: As per the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) guidelines, what is the minimum net worth requirement for a non-bank entity to set up, own, and operate White Label ATMs (WLAs) in India?
questions.explanation: According to the RBI guidelines on White Label ATMs (WLAs), non-bank entities that intend to set up and operate WLAs in India must have a minimum net worth of ₹100 crore, which must be maintained at all times.

questions.question: Under the Sovereign Gold Bond (SGB) Scheme issued by the RBI, what is the maximum annual subscription limit per fiscal year (April–March) for Hindu Undivided Families (HUFs) and Trusts respectively?
questions.explanation: Under the Sovereign Gold Bond (SGB) Scheme, the maximum limit of subscription per fiscal year (April-March) is 4 kg for individuals, 4 kg for Hindu Undivided Families (HUFs), and 20 kg for trusts and similar entities.

questions.question: An escalator is moving downwards at a constant speed. Amit walks down the escalator and takes 40 steps to reach the bottom. Binita runs down the same escalator and takes 60 steps to reach the bottom. If Binita's speed of walking/running is twice that of Amit, how many steps are visible on the escalator when it is stationary?
questions.explanation: Let the speed of Amit be 'a' steps per second. Since Binita's speed is twice that of Amit, Binita's speed is '2a' steps per second. Let the speed of the escalator be 'e' steps per second. Time taken by Amit = 40/a. During this time, the escalator covers e * (40/a) steps. So, total steps N = 40 + 40(e/a). Time taken by Binita = 60/(2a) = 30/a. During this time, the escalator covers e * (30/a) steps. So, total steps N = 60 + 30(e/a). Equating the two expressions for N: 40 + 40(e/a) = 60 + 30(e/a) => 10(e/a) = 20 => e/a = 2. Substituting e/a = 2 into the first equation: N = 40 + 40(2) = 120 steps.

questions.question: A certain sum of money is invested at a rate of r% per annum simple interest for 3 years. The same sum is invested at the same rate of r% per annum compound interest (compounded annually) for 2 years. If the ratio of the simple interest earned in 3 years to the compound interest earned in 2 years is 15 : 11, find the value of r.
questions.explanation: Let the Principal be P and x = r/100. Simple Interest (SI) for 3 years = P * x * 3 = 3Px. Compound Interest (CI) for 2 years = P * [(1 + x)^2 - 1] = P * (x^2 + 2x). The ratio of SI to CI is given as 15/11. Therefore, 3Px / [P * (x^2 + 2x)] = 15/11 => 3 / (x + 2) = 15/11 => 33 = 15x + 30 => 15x = 3 => x = 3/15 = 1/5 = 0.2. Thus, r = 0.2 * 100 = 20%.

questions.question: In a family of seven members - P, Q, R, S, T, U, and V - there are two married couples. P is the mother-in-law of T. T is married to R. S is the brother of R. V is the father of S. U is the son of R, and Q is the daughter of T. How is S related to Q?
questions.explanation: P is the mother-in-law of T, who is married to R, meaning P is R's mother. S is the brother of R, and V is the father of S (and therefore of R as well). U and Q are the children of R and T. Since S is the brother of R (Q's parent), S is the uncle of Q.

questions.question:
Read the statements carefully and decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements, disregarding commonly known facts.

Statements:
1. Only a few books are novels.
2. All novels are stories.
3. No story is a cartoon.

Conclusions:
I. Some books are not novels.
II. Some novels being cartoons is a possibility.
III. All books can never be cartoons.
options: All I, II and III follow
questions.explanation: Conclusion I follows because 'Only a few books are novels' directly implies that some books are not novels. Conclusion II does not follow because all novels are stories, and no story is a cartoon, which means no novel can ever be a cartoon. Conclusion III follows because some books are novels (and hence are stories), and since no story is a cartoon, those books can never be cartoons, meaning all books can never be cartoons.

questions.question:
Complete the sentence by choosing the most appropriate pair of words:

Given the _________ nature of the global market, the board decided to adopt a _________ approach toward capital expenditure this quarter.
questions.explanation: The word 'volatile' means highly unstable or unpredictable, which logically prompts a 'conservative' (cautious and risk-averse) approach to capital expenditure. The other options are either contradictory or logically inconsistent in a corporate decision-making context.

questions.question:
Identify the grammatically correct option to replace the bold phrase in the sentence:

'No sooner had the regulatory body cleared the merger **when the shares of both companies surged** to record highs.'
options: than the shares of both companies surged
then the shares of both companies surged
when the shares of both companies had surged
than the shares of both companies had surged
questions.explanation: The correlative conjunction 'No sooner' must always be paired with 'than' (not 'when' or 'then'). Additionally, when 'No sooner' introduces a past event, the standard structure is 'No sooner had + subject + past participle... than + subject + simple past'. Therefore, 'than the shares of both companies surged' is correct.

questions.question: Under the Prompt Corrective Action (PCA) framework for NBFCs introduced by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) which came into effect on October 1, 2022, which of the following financial parameters is NOT monitored as a key indicator for assessing the trigger points?
questions.explanation: The RBI's PCA framework for NBFCs (specifically for deposit-taking and non-deposit-taking middle, upper, and top-layer NBFCs) monitors three key performance indicators: Capital (CRAR and Common Equity Tier 1), Asset Quality (Net NPA Ratio), and Leverage (Tier I Leverage Ratio). Return on Assets (RoA) is not used as a trigger indicator under this framework.

questions.question: Under Section 45ZB of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Act, 1934, the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members. Which of the following statements is correct regarding the members appointed by the Central Government?
options: There are three government-appointed members, holding office for a term of 3 years and eligible for re-appointment.
There are three government-appointed members, holding office for a term of 4 years and not eligible for re-appointment.
There are three government-appointed members, holding office for a term of 5 years and not eligible for re-appointment.
There are two government-appointed members, holding office for a term of 4 years and eligible for re-appointment.
questions.explanation: According to the RBI Act, 1934, the Central Government appoints three members to the MPC. These members hold office for a period of four years and are not eligible for re-appointment.

questions.question: A boat covers a distance of 48 km downstream in 3 hours. If the ratio of the speed of the boat in still water to the speed of the stream is 5 : 3, how much time (in hours) will the boat take to cover a distance of 32 km upstream?
questions.explanation: Downstream speed (D) = Distance / Time = 48 / 3 = 16 km/h. Let the speed of the boat in still water be 5k and the speed of the stream be 3k. Therefore, downstream speed = 5k + 3k = 8k. Since 8k = 16 km/h, we get k = 2. The speed of the boat in still water is 5 * 2 = 10 km/h, and the speed of the stream is 3 * 2 = 6 km/h. The upstream speed (U) = 10 - 6 = 4 km/h. The time taken to cover 32 km upstream is 32 / 4 = 8 hours.

questions.question: A solid metallic cylinder of base radius 10 cm and height 18 cm is melted and recast into 'n' small solid spheres of radius 3 cm each. If 10% of the metal is wasted in the melting and recasting process, what is the value of 'n'?
questions.explanation: The volume of the original cylinder is V = π * r^2 * h = π * 10^2 * 18 = 1800π cm³. Since 10% of the metal is wasted, the usable volume is 90% of 1800π = 1620π cm³. The volume of one small sphere of radius 3 cm is v = (4/3) * π * 3^3 = 36π cm³. The number of spheres 'n' is given by (usable volume) / (volume of one sphere) = 1620π / 36π = 45.

questions.question:
In a certain coded language, the following symbols represent relationships:
'P # Q' means 'P is the father of Q'
'P + Q' means 'P is the mother of Q'
'P - Q' means 'P is the brother of Q'
'P % Q' means 'P is the wife of Q'

If the expression 'A + B - C % D # E' is true, how is A related to E?
questions.explanation:
From the expression 'A + B - C % D # E':
1. 'A + B' means A is the mother of B (Female).
2. 'B - C' means B is the brother of C (Male). Thus, A is also the mother of C.
3. 'C % D' means C is the wife of D (Female). This makes D the husband of C.
4. 'D # E' means D is the father of E.
Since C is the wife of D and D is the father of E, C must be the mother of E. Since A is the mother of C, A is the mother's mother (Maternal Grandmother) of E.

questions.question:
Read the statements carefully and decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements, disregarding commonly known facts.

Statements:
1. Only a few Gliders are Parachutes.
2. No Parachute is a Balloon.
3. All Balloons are Hot-airs.

Conclusions:
I. Some Gliders are definitely not Balloons.
II. Some Hot-airs being Parachutes is a possibility.
options: Both conclusion I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows
questions.explanation:
1. 'Only a few Gliders are Parachutes' implies 'Some Gliders are Parachutes' and 'Some Gliders are not Parachutes'.
2. Since some Gliders are Parachutes, and no Parachute is a Balloon, those Gliders which are Parachutes can never be Balloons. Thus, 'Some Gliders are definitely not Balloons' (Conclusion I) is definitely true.
3. 'No Parachute is a Balloon' and 'All Balloons are Hot-airs' allows the part of Hot-airs that is not Balloons to overlap with Parachutes. Thus, 'Some Hot-airs being Parachutes is a possibility' (Conclusion II) is also true.

questions.question:
In the following sentence, four words have been marked in bold and labeled (A), (B), (C), and (D). Choose the option that represents the correct interchange of these words to make the sentence grammatically and contextually correct:

"The **accountability** (A) objective of the new policy is to **enhance** (B) transparency and **primary** (C) in the banking **sector** (D)."
questions.explanation: The words 'accountability' and 'primary' need to be interchanged. 'Primary' is an adjective that correctly modifies the noun 'objective' ('primary objective'), and 'accountability' is a noun that fits parallelly with 'transparency' ('transparency and accountability'). Thus, exchanging A and C makes the sentence grammatically and contextually correct.

questions.question:
In the following sentence, one of the parts may contain a grammatical error. Select the part that contains the error. If the sentence is correct, select 'No error'.

"Scarcely had the new tax policy (A) / been implemented than the retail sector (B) / witnessed a sharp decline in consumer spending. (C) / No error (D)"
options: Scarcely had the new tax policy
been implemented than the retail sector
witnessed a sharp decline in consumer spending
questions.explanation: The conjunction 'scarcely' is grammatically paired with 'when' or 'before' to introduce the second clause, whereas 'than' is strictly paired with 'no sooner'. Therefore, 'than' in part (B) must be replaced with 'when' to correct the error.

questions.question: Under the Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) guidelines for 'on-tap' licensing of Small Finance Banks (SFBs) in the private sector, what is the minimum paid-up voting equity capital required?
questions.explanation: As per the RBI's 'on-tap' licensing guidelines for Small Finance Banks issued in December 2019, the minimum paid-up voting equity capital or net worth requirement was increased from the earlier ₹100 crore to ₹200 crore.

questions.question: According to the Reserve Bank of India (Internal Ombudsman for Non-Banking Financial Companies) Directions, 2021, which of the following categories of NBFCs is required to appoint an Internal Ombudsman?
options: All deposit-taking NBFCs irrespective of their branch network size
Non-deposit-taking NBFCs with asset size of ₹5,000 crore and above and having public customer interface
All NBFCs registered as Infrastructure Debt Funds (IDF-NBFCs)
Systemically Important Non-Deposit taking NBFCs with asset size of ₹1,000 crore and above
questions.explanation: The RBI directions on Internal Ombudsman for NBFCs (2021) mandate the appointment of an Internal Ombudsman for deposit-taking NBFCs with 10 or more branches, and non-deposit-taking NBFCs with an asset size of ₹5,000 crore and above that have a public customer interface.

questions.question: In December 2023, the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) enhanced the transaction limit for Unified Payments Interface (UPI) payments to hospitals and educational institutions to what amount?
questions.explanation: In its December 2023 Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting, the Reserve Bank of India announced an increase in the UPI transaction limit for payments to hospitals and educational institutions from ₹1 Lakh to ₹5 Lakh per transaction.

questions.question: As per the updated Reserve Bank of India (RBI) guidelines, what is the minimum denomination in which a Certificate of Deposit (CD) can be issued, and what is its minimum maturity period when issued by a scheduled commercial bank?
questions.explanation: According to the RBI Master Direction on Money Market Instruments, Certificates of Deposit (CDs) must be issued in a minimum denomination of ₹5 Lakh and in multiples of ₹5 Lakh thereafter. The maturity period of CDs issued by banks must not be less than 7 days and not more than 1 year.

questions.question: There are 6 letters to be delivered to 6 different addresses. If they are randomly put into 6 addressed envelopes, what is the probability that exactly 4 letters are delivered to the correct addresses?
questions.explanation: To find the probability that exactly 4 letters are delivered to the correct addresses, we need to choose which 4 letters are correct and ensure the remaining 2 are incorrect (deranged). The number of ways to choose 4 correct letters out of 6 is C(6, 4) = 15. The remaining 2 letters must both go into the wrong envelopes. The number of derangements for 2 items is D(2) = 1. Therefore, the number of favorable outcomes is 15 * 1 = 15. The total number of ways to arrange 6 letters in 6 envelopes is 6! = 720. Thus, the probability is 15/720 = 1/48.

questions.question: A sum of Rs. X is invested in Scheme A which offers 15% per annum simple interest. After 2 years, the total amount received from Scheme A is reinvested in Scheme B which offers 10% per annum compound interest (compounded annually) for 2 years. If the total interest earned from Scheme B is Rs. 1,365, find the value of X.
questions.explanation:
Let the principal be X.
Amount received from Scheme A after 2 years at 15% SI = X + (X * 15 * 2)/100 = X + 0.30X = 1.30X.
This amount (1.30X) is invested in Scheme B at 10% CI for 2 years.
The effective interest rate of 10% CI for 2 years is 10 + 10 + (10 * 10)/100 = 21%.
Interest earned from Scheme B = 21% of 1.30X = 0.21 * 1.30X = 0.273X.
Given: 0.273X = 1365
=> X = 1365 / 0.273 = 5000.
Therefore, the value of X is Rs. 5,000.

questions.question:
Statements:
Only a few Roses are Jasmine.
No Jasmine is Tulip.
All Tulips are Lotus.

Conclusions:
I. Some Roses are definitely not Tulips.
II. All Lotus being Jasmine is a possibility.

Which of the following options is correct?
options: Both conclusion I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows
questions.explanation:
1. 'Only a few Roses are Jasmine' implies both 'Some Roses are Jasmine' and 'Some Roses are not Jasmine'.
2. Since some Roses are Jasmine, and 'No Jasmine is Tulip', those Roses which are Jasmine can never be Tulips. Thus, 'Some Roses are definitely not Tulips' (Conclusion I) is definitely true.
3. 'All Tulips are Lotus' and 'No Jasmine is Tulip' means that the part of Lotus which is Tulip can never be Jasmine. Thus, all Lotus can never be Jasmine, making 'All Lotus being Jasmine is a possibility' (Conclusion II) false.

questions.question:
In a certain code language:
'secure monetary policy growth' is coded as 'S#22 M#02 P@02 G#19'
'rate demand analysis trend' is coded as 'R@22 D@23 A#08 T#23'
'banking system sector reform' is coded as 'B@20 S#14 S@09 R@14'

What is the code for the word 'development' in this code language?
questions.explanation:
The logic behind the code is:
1. The first letter of the code is the same as the first letter of the word (for 'development', it is 'D').
2. The symbol is '@' if the number of vowels in the word is even, and '#' if the number of vowels is odd. In 'development', the vowels are 'e, e, o, e' (4 vowels, which is even), so the symbol is '@'.
3. The number represents the positional value of the letter opposite to the last letter of the word in alphabetical order. The last letter of 'development' is 't' (20th letter), and its opposite letter is 'g' (7th letter), so the number is 07.
Combining these gives 'D@07'.

questions.question:
In the following sentence, four words have been printed in bold and labeled A, B, C, and D. These words may or may not be in their correct positions. Find the correct combination of words that need to be swapped to make the sentence grammatically correct and meaningful.

"The **sustainable** (A) effects of climate change have **sectors** (B) the need for **disruptive** (C) practices across all **heightened** (D) of the economy."
questions.explanation: The word 'sustainable' in position (A) is contextually incorrect because the effects of climate change are 'disruptive' rather than sustainable. Thus, A and C need to be swapped to get 'disruptive effects' and 'sustainable practices'. Similarly, 'sectors' in position (B) is a noun and cannot follow the auxiliary verb 'have' in this context. Swapping B and D gives 'have heightened' (present perfect verb) and 'all sectors of the economy' (noun phrase), making the sentence grammatically correct and meaningful.

questions.question:
Identify the part of the sentence that contains a grammatical error. If there is no error, select the option indicating no error.

"The municipal corporation has issued (A) / a strict advisory to the residents (B) / to keep their windows closed (C) / lest the toxic fumes will cause respiratory issues (D)."
questions.explanation: The conjunction 'lest' means 'for fear that' or 'to avoid the risk of'. It is grammatically followed by a subjunctive clause (using the base form of the verb) or by 'should' + base verb. It cannot be followed by auxiliaries like 'will', 'would', 'may', or 'can'. Therefore, 'will cause' in Part D must be replaced with 'should cause' or 'cause' to be correct.

questions.question: Under the revised Liquidity Adjustment Facility (LAF) framework introduced by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) in April 2022, the Standing Deposit Facility (SDF) rate is set at how many basis points below the Policy Repo Rate?
questions.explanation: In April 2022, the RBI introduced the Standing Deposit Facility (SDF) as the floor of the LAF corridor, replacing the fixed reverse repo rate. The SDF rate is aligned at 25 basis points (0.25%) below the Policy Repo Rate.

questions.question: To prevent corporate debtors, particularly MSMEs, from being pushed into insolvency proceedings due to temporary economic stress, the minimum threshold of default for initiating the Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process (CIRP) under the IBC, 2016 was increased to what amount?
questions.explanation: In March 2020, the Government of India exercised its powers under Section 4 of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC), 2016, to increase the minimum threshold of default from Rs. 1 Lakh to Rs. 1 Crore in order to protect businesses from being dragged into insolvency.

questions.question: An urn contains 6 red, 4 blue, and 5 green balls. If three balls are drawn at random, what is the probability that at least two of the drawn balls are of the same color?
questions.explanation: Total number of balls = 6 + 4 + 5 = 15. The number of ways to draw 3 balls out of 15 is 15C3 = (15 * 14 * 13) / (3 * 2 * 1) = 455. The event 'at least two balls are of the same color' is the complement of the event 'all three balls are of different colors'. The number of ways to choose exactly one ball of each color is 6C1 * 4C1 * 5C1 = 6 * 4 * 5 = 120. Thus, the probability of getting all three different colors is 120/455 = 24/91. Therefore, the probability of getting at least two balls of the same color is 1 - 24/91 = 67/91.

questions.question: A person invested a sum of Rs. P in Scheme A offering 20% p.a. compound interest (compounded annually) for 3 years. He invested another sum, which is Rs. 4,000 more than P, in Scheme B offering 10% p.a. simple interest for 3 years. If the interest earned from Scheme A is Rs. 3,080 more than the interest earned from Scheme B, find the total sum invested by the person in both schemes together.
questions.explanation: Interest from Scheme A (CI for 3 years at 20%): I_A = P * [(1 + 0.20)^3 - 1] = P * [1.728 - 1] = 0.728P. Interest from Scheme B (SI for 3 years at 10% on P + 4000): I_B = (P + 4000) * 10% * 3 = 0.30(P + 4000) = 0.30P + 1200. Given that I_A - I_B = 3080. Substituting the values: 0.728P - (0.30P + 1200) = 3080 => 0.428P - 1200 = 3080 => 0.428P = 4280 => P = 10,000. The total sum invested in both schemes is P + (P + 4000) = 10,000 + 14,000 = Rs. 24,000.

questions.question:
Statements:
Only a few oceans are seas.
All seas are rivers.
No river is a lake.

Conclusions:
I. All oceans being rivers is a possibility.
II. Some seas are not lakes.
options: Both conclusion I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows
questions.explanation:
1. 'Only a few oceans are seas' means 'Some oceans are seas' and 'Some oceans are not seas'. Since 'All seas are rivers', the oceans that are not seas can still be part of rivers. Thus, all oceans being rivers is a valid possibility, so Conclusion I follows.
2. Since 'All seas are rivers' and 'No river is a lake', it is certain that 'No sea is a lake'. If no sea is a lake, then 'Some seas are not lakes' is also definitely true. Thus, Conclusion II follows.

questions.question:
In the following question, the symbols $, @, %, #, and * are used with the following meanings:
'P $ Q' means 'P is neither smaller than nor equal to Q'
'P @ Q' means 'P is neither greater than nor equal to Q'
'P % Q' means 'P is not greater than Q'
'P # Q' means 'P is not smaller than Q'
'P * Q' means 'P is neither greater than nor smaller than Q'

Statements: H # T, T @ F, F % L, L * W

Conclusions:
I. W $ T
II. H @ F
options: Both conclusions I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows
questions.explanation:
Decoding the symbols:
$ means >
@ means <
% means <=
# means >=
* means =

Converting the statements:
H >= T, T < F, F <= L, L = W
Combining them: H >= T < F <= L = W

Evaluating Conclusions:
I. W $ T => W > T. From the chain T < F <= L = W, we clearly get T < W (or W > T). Hence, I is true.
II. H @ F => H < F. From H >= T < F, the signs are in opposite directions between H and F, so no relationship can be established. Hence, II is false.

questions.question:
In the following question, two sentences are given with a blank in each. Select the word from the given options that fits grammatically and contextually in both the blanks.

I. Several environmental organizations have decided to ________ the new green energy initiative proposed by the ministry.
II. Known as a fierce ________ of free speech, the journalist refused to reveal her sources despite intense political pressure.
questions.explanation: 'Champion' can act as both a verb and a noun. In sentence I, 'to champion' acts as an infinitive verb meaning to support or defend a cause. In sentence II, 'champion' acts as a noun meaning a person who vigorously supports or defends a cause. 'Bolster' and 'defend' do not fit grammatically as nouns in sentence II without modification, and 'proponent' cannot be used as a verb in sentence I.

questions.question:
Four of the five sentences (A, B, C, D, and E) given below can be rearranged to form a coherent paragraph. Identify the sentence that does NOT fit into the sequence and is the 'odd one out'.

(A) While central banks globally are raising interest rates to combat inflation, the domestic market has shown remarkable resilience due to robust consumer demand.
(B) This resilience is particularly visible in the automotive and real estate sectors, which have recorded double-digit growth over the last two quarters.
(C) Economists suggest that sustained wage growth and a stable job market have acted as a buffer against external macroeconomic shocks.
(D) Consequently, foreign institutional investors have turned net buyers, pumping billions of dollars into Indian equities.
(E) However, the transition to renewable energy sources remains painfully slow due to policy bottlenecks and high capital costs.
questions.explanation: Sentences A, B, C, and D form a logically coherent paragraph discussing the resilience of the domestic market, the sectors driving it, the underlying economic factors (wage and job stability), and the resulting foreign investment. Sentence E introduces an entirely unrelated topic—the transition to renewable energy and its policy bottlenecks—making it the odd one out.

questions.question: Under the Liberalised Remittance Scheme (LRS) of the Reserve Bank of India, what is the maximum permissible limit for resident individuals to freely remit funds abroad per financial year (April-March) for any permitted current or capital account transaction?
questions.explanation: Under the Liberalised Remittance Scheme (LRS), all resident individuals, including minors, are allowed to freely remit up to USD 250,000 per financial year (April - March) for any permissible current or capital account transaction or a combination of both.

questions.question: Under the four-tier regulatory framework for Urban Co-operative Banks (UCBs) implemented by the Reserve Bank of India, Tier 1 UCBs include unit UCBs and salary earners' UCBs, as well as other UCBs with deposits up to which of the following thresholds?
questions.explanation: According to the RBI's revised regulatory framework for UCBs, Tier 1 comprises unit UCBs and salary earners' UCBs (irrespective of deposit size), and all other UCBs having deposits up to ₹100 crore.
